D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Homer's "Wine-Dark Sea" — Why No Word for Blue?

Homer described the sea as "wine-dark" (oínops) and sheep as "violet" — Gladstone 1858 catalogued the absence of "blue" in Homeric Greek, sparking the entire field of colour-term anthropology

In ~80,000 lines of Iliad and Odyssey, Homer never describes the sea as "blue". The sea is "wine-dark", "violet", "grey" — never the modern Greek κυάνεος. Gladstone (PM of UK!) wrote a 200-page essay in 1858 arguing the Greeks were colour-blind. The real answer: their language lacked a basic "blue" term — they were at Berlin-Kay Stage IV.

Pick an epithet; the Aegean Sea is rendered in that colour as Homer apparently described it.
